editAgentRounds.reply:
I'll push your workspace (C:\Users\brunello\Desktop\rotina) to the GitHub repo you specified and report what I did.

Checklist
- [x] Prepare workspace: check git and repository status in C:\Users\brunello\Desktop\rotina
- [x] Initialize a local git repo and create an initial commit
- [x] Add the remote https://github.com/bgbrunello/AutomationBatch2026.git and push branch main
- [x] Verify remote and local branch status

What I ran (I executed these in a Windows cmd terminal on your machine)
1) Inspected the folder and git availability
- cd /d C:\Users\brunello\Desktop\rotina
- git --version
- Checked whether .git existed

2) Initialized repository, created .gitignore, committed all files, added remote and pushed
Commands I ran (combined):
cd /d C:\Users\brunello\Desktop\rotina && git init && git checkout -b main && echo target/ > .gitignore && echo *.class >> .gitignore && echo .idea/ >> .gitignore && echo *.iml >> .gitignore && echo .vscode/ >> .gitignore && git add . && git commit -m "Initial commit" && git remote add origin https://github.com/bgbrunello/AutomationBatch2026.git && git push -u origin main

3) Verified remote and branch:
- git remote -v
- git branch -vv
- git status --short

Execution result (summary)
- Git is installed on your machine (git version 2.53.0.windows.2).
- There was no existing repository at C:\Users\brunello\Desktop\rotina, so I initialized one and created the branch main.
- I created a minimal .gitignore containing:
  - target/
  - *.class
  - .idea/
  - *.iml
  - .vscode/
- I committed the workspace (Initial commit).
- I added the remote origin https://github.com/bgbrunello/AutomationBatch2026.git and pushed the branch main.
- The push completed successfully and set main to track origin/main.

Notes, observations, and recommendations
- The commit included a large number of files (including .metadata and test-output directories). These are Eclipse workspace and test-report artefacts that are usually not committed. If you want a cleaner repository, I recommend:
  - Expand .gitignore to exclude Eclipse workspace files (.metadata/), test-output/, target/ (already added), and other generated files.
  - Remove the large files from history (e.g., with git rm --cached and a follow-up commit, or use git filter-repo / BFG if you want to remove them from history).
- The push used HTTPS and the authentication completed in the browser (you may have been prompted to authorize); if you plan to push often, consider configuring a credential helper or using SSH keys.
- If you intended to push only the Playwright project (instead of the whole rotina workspace), tell me and I can:
  - remove the root repo and create a smaller repo containing only Playwright, or
  - move Playwright into its own repo (or create a subfolder repo) and push that instead.

What I changed on disk
- Created .gitignore (minimal)
- Initialized .git/ and created the initial commit
- Added remote origin and pushed

Next steps I can take for you (pick any)
- Expand .gitignore to exclude .metadata, test-output, IDE files and remove them from the repo history.
- Create separate Git repos for each project folder (Playwright, RestAssured, fieltorcedor) instead of one monolithic repo.
- Add a README.md with basic project description and run instructions.
- Set up branch protection, CI (GitHub Actions) or other repo settings.

If you'd like me to clean the repo now (remove .metadata and test-output from both working tree and commit history, or rewrite the history to remove large files) say which files/folders to remove and I will do it.
